# Reviewer Usability Checklist: Maintainer and Contributor Release Workflow

**Purpose**: Validate that DOC-009 requirements make the page usable for contributors preparing PRs, maintainers checking release readiness, and reviewers evaluating evidence.
**Created**: 2026-06-18
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` phase based on feature context and requirements.

## Contributor Readiness

- [x] CHK001 Does the contributor path require guidance for choosing the smallest source surface before editing generated output? [Spec §FR-005, Design Concept §Goals]
- [x] CHK002 Does the spec require validation evidence in the PR body for each relevant change type? [Spec §FR-004, Spec §FR-005]
- [x] CHK003 Does the change-type matrix give contributors enough information to identify source surfaces, generated/synchronized surfaces, required checks, and review evidence? [Spec §FR-004, Spec §AC-9.1]

## Maintainer Readiness

- [x] CHK004 Does the maintainer path require source/dist parity, Claude/Codex marketplace parity, manifest version consistency, generated payload validation, full shell suite, and docs-site validation when relevant? [Spec §FR-006, Spec §AC-9.4]
- [x] CHK005 Does the final checklist requirement cover all DOC-009 acceptance criteria? [Spec §FR-015, Spec §SC-006]
- [x] CHK006 Does the plan keep maintainer guidance source-backed without forcing readers to reconstruct the workflow from all of `CLAUDE.md`? [Plan §Summary, Spec §FR-012]

## Reviewer PR Metadata

- [x] CHK007 Does the spec require Conventional Commit PR titles? [Spec §FR-005, Spec §AC-9.5]
- [x] CHK008 Does the spec require public-readable PR body expectations, including what changed, why, non-goals, review order, scope budget, traceability, verification, known gaps, and rollback/flags? [Spec §PR Review Packet Requirements, Plan §Reviewability and PR Packet Plan]
- [x] CHK009 Are reviewer success criteria measurable enough to support task generation and final review? [Spec §SC-003, Spec §SC-006]

## Findings

- No gap markers found.
- No consensus escalation required.
